TORNADO SPUN UP JUST NORTHWEST OF THE VILLAGE OF STURTEVANT ABOUT 120 PM AND MOVED EAST-SOUTHEAST THROUGH THE NORTHERN PART OF STURTEVANT. IT DISSIPATED JUST WEST OF ELMWOOD PARK AT ABOUT 128 PM. IT DAMAGED TWO DOORS OF THE STURTEVANT FIRE DEPARTMENT...AND DAMAGED THE ROOFS OF TWO HOMES. ONE HOME HAD A COUPLE WINDOWS BLOWN IN. AT A BUSINESS...IT FLIPPED AND MOVED TWO TRAILERS AB0UT 50 YARDS AND SNAPPED 3 LIGHT POLES. IT ALSO DAMAGED A TRAMPOLINE. ABOUT A DOZEN TREES WERE UPROOTED AND AT LEAST A COULE DOZEN OTHER TREES HAD BROKEN LIMBS. THE MAXIMUM WINDS WERE IN THE 95 TO 100 MPH RANGE. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS ABOUT 100 YARDS. THE PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT 4.5 MILES. THE RESPONSIBLE THUNDERSTORM MOVED SOUTHEAST OVER LAKE MICHIGAN ABOUT 1 MILE NORTH-NORTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF STH 32 AND CTH KR...AND MAY HAVE GENERATED A WATERSPOUT OVER THE LAKE. OVER THE EXTREME SOUTHEAST CORNER OF KENOSHA COUNTY AND OVER NORTHEASTERN KENOSHA COUNTY THERE WAS SCATTERED TREE DAMAGE DUE TO REAR FLANK DOWNDRAFT WINDS. DAMAGE SURVEY DONE IN CONJUNCTION WITH THE RACINE COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT DIRECTOR.

TORNADO LIKELY TOUCHED DOWN A FEW HUNDRED YARDS NORTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 11 AND 90TH STREET AT AROUND 122 PM CDT. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED EAST SOUTHEAST...NEARLY FOLLOWING HWY 11. THE TOTAL PATH LENGTH WAS CLOSE TO 3 MILES...AND THE TORNADO LIFTED CLOSE TO THE 3500 BLOCK OF MEACHEM ROAD IN ELMWOOD PARK AROUND 127 PM CDT. THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS BETWEEN 75 AND 100 YARDS AND THE MAXIMUM WIND GUST WAS BETWEEN 80 AND 85 MPH. THERE WAS MINOR DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES AND TREE DAMAGE.
TREES DOWN ON GREEN RIDGE DRIVE AND SOUTHWOOD DRIVE. FUNNEL CLOUD ALSO REPORTED IN THE AREA.
FLOODING AT INTERSECTION OF HWY K AND THE FRONTAGE ROAD OF I-94. REPORT OF NUMEROUS STALLED CARS...ONE AERIAL LADDER RESCUE REQUIRED.

TORNADO STARTED 2 MILES SOUTHWEST OF CAMBRIA AT 627 PM. IT MOVED EAST NORTHEAST FROM THERE. IT MAY HAVE TRACKED TO JUST NORTH OF FOX LAKE IN DODGE COUNTY. DAMAGE INCLUDED A COUPLE OF BARNS DOWN IN THE AREA OF THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY HIGHWAY B AND JONES DRIVE. OTHER DAMAGE REPORTED AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 146 AND CEMETERY ROAD. DAMAGE SURVEY WILL BE CONDUCTED BY THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE IN SULLIVAN IN THIS AREA ON THURSDAY.

WEAK EF0 TORNADO AFFECTED THE SOUTHEAST PART OF THE CITY OF VERONA FOR ABOUT A MINUTE. IT WAS VIDEO TAPED BY 3 PEOPLE. VIDEO TAPE EVIDENCE IN CONJUNCTION WITH VERBAL DESCRIPTIONS CLEARLY INDICATED THAT A TORNADO OCCURRED. THE SO-CALLED FUNNEL CLOUD DID NOT EXTEND ALL THE WAY TO THE GROUND...BUT THERE WAS GROUND CIRCULATION. DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO TREE BRANCHES IN THE VICINITY OF WHALEN ROAD WHERE IT IS BOUNDED BY CTH M AND STH 151/18. WIND SPEEDS WERE PROBABLY ON THE ORDER OF 65 TO 70 MPH.
